pulse: US equity markets closed the week at fresh records on Friday May 1 with the S&P 500 at 7,230 and Nasdaq at 25,114, lifted by Apple Q2 earnings that crushed estimates and an AI-driven tech rally. The dominant overhang is the US-Iran war on Day 63: Iran submitted a revised peace proposal to Pakistani mediators but Trump signalled rejection, keeping Brent above 111 dollars and the Strait of Hormuz risk premium in prices. The Fed held rates at 3.75% on April 29 in its most contested vote since 1992, and Jerome Powell chair term expires May 15 with Kevin Warsh advancing toward Senate confirmation.

movers.name: RDDT
movers.reason: Reddit surged on bullish Q1 revenue and upbeat forward guidance on May 1, confirming its advertising monetisation flywheel is accelerating.

movers.name: RBLX
movers.reason: Roblox cratered after slashing full-year 2026 bookings guidance from 6.02-6.29B to 5.87-6.14B at the top end, sending a chill through gaming names.

movers.name: AAPL
movers.reason: Apple beat Q2 EPS of 2.01 versus 1.96 estimate on revenue of 111.2B; iPhone 17 driving 22% iPhone revenue growth with Q3 guidance of 14-17% crushing 9% consensus.

movers.name: BRENT
movers.reason: Brent crude held above 111 per barrel as Trump rejected Iran revised ceasefire proposal, keeping Strait of Hormuz closure fears front and centre.

portfolio: Marc SGD-base portfolio navigates a binary macro moment. Gold at 4613 anchors the book with geopolitical support from the Iran war. Oil positions benefit from Brent above 111 but face sharp reversion risk on any ceasefire breakthrough. US equity exposure is well-positioned with Nasdaq at all-time highs on Apple and AI. IBEX35 at 17781 constructive on Spain GDP outperformance and unemployment below 10%, but Trump trade war threat over Spain military base refusal is a tail risk. Singapore positions benefit from Q1 GDP 4.6% and MTI upgraded 2-4% 2026 forecast. 9G2.SI (Singapore Institute of Advanced Medicine Holdings) fell 8.7% on April 30 to S$0.042 and is down 39% YTD - requires urgent position review. BTC at 78324 with Fear and Greed at 43 lacks a near-term catalyst; ETF inflows provide a floor. SGD firm on Singapore macro strength.

alerts.text: 9G2.SI down 8.7% on April 30 to S$0.042; -39% YTD. Sustained drawdown warrants urgent position review.
Powell chairmanship expires May 15. Warsh era begins - historic 4-member FOMC dissent, most since 1992.
Iran ceasefire talks critical: Trump rejected revised proposal May 1. Risk of war resumption keeps oil elevated and Strait of Hormuz closed.
Trump threatened trade war with Spain after Madrid refused US use of military bases for Iran operations. IBEX35 tail risk.
War Powers Act 60-day deadline hit May 1. Senate blocked resolution 47-50. Congressional-executive tension escalating.
Roblox guidance cut caused 24% crash. Gaming and virtual world names at risk.
Apple Q2 beat: 111.2B revenue up 17%, EPS 2.01. Q3 guidance 14-17% crushed 9% consensus. Tim Cook becomes Executive Chairman September 1, John Ternus becomes CEO.
Berkshire first annual meeting under Greg Abel as CEO held May 2. Buffett 95 remains chairman.
US April nonfarm payrolls due Friday May 8. March was +178,000, unemployment 4.3%.
Anthropic 900B-plus valuation fundraising round expected within 2 weeks. Google committing up to 40B.

news.title: Iran submits fresh ceasefire proposal; Trump signals rejection
news.text: On May 1 - Day 63 of the US-Iran war - Iran sent a revised peace proposal to Pakistani mediators before a key deadline. Trump said he was not satisfied and is being briefed on military strike options. Brent held above 111 dollars as markets priced continued Strait of Hormuz closure risk.

news.title: War Powers Act 60-day deadline passes; Senate blocks resolution 47-50
news.text: May 1 marked 60 days since Trump notified Congress of Iran operations. Senate blocked a war powers resolution demanding Congressional approval by 47-50, handing Trump a legislative win but deepening constitutional tension.

news.title: Fed holds at 3.75% in most contested vote since 1992; Warsh advancing
news.text: The FOMC voted April 29 to hold rates at 3.5-3.75% with four dissenting members - largest dissent since 1992. Powell statement cited Middle East developments and high uncertainty. Chairmanship expires May 15; Warsh Senate Banking Committee nomination advanced 13-11.

news.title: Apple posts 111.2B Q2 revenue; Cook hands CEO role to Ternus in September
news.text: Apple fiscal Q2 2026 showed revenue up 17% to 111.2B beating 109.7B estimate. EPS 2.01 beat 1.96. iPhone revenue 57B up 22%. Q3 guidance 14-17% crushed 9% consensus. Tim Cook becomes Executive Chairman September 1 with hardware chief John Ternus named CEO.

news.title: Nasdaq and S&P 500 at fresh all-time highs; April best month since 2020
news.text: Nasdaq closed at 25114 and S&P 500 at 7230 on May 1, both new record closes. April was the best month for US equities since November 2020 with S&P gaining around 10%.

news.title: BP Q1 profit more than doubles to 3.2B on Iran war oil trading
news.text: BP reported Q1 2026 underlying profit of 3.2B up from 1.38B a year earlier and above 2.63B analyst estimate, crediting exceptional oil trading amid Hormuz disruption. BP shares up 32% year to date.

news.title: Berkshire first shareholder meeting under Greg Abel draws tens of thousands
news.text: Berkshire Hathaway held its first annual meeting under CEO Greg Abel on May 2 in Omaha with Warren Buffett 95 present as chairman. Abel uses his entire 15M after-tax annual salary to buy Berkshire shares. Berkshire down 5% YTD.

news.title: Aung San Suu Kyi transferred to house arrest after five-plus years in prison
news.text: Myanmar junta transferred opposition leader Aung San Suu Kyi from prison to house arrest on May 1, more than five years after the 2021 coup. Analysts read the move as a possible signal of quiet back-channel negotiations.

news.title: Zelenskyy seeks terms of Russia short-term ceasefire proposal to US
news.text: Ukrainian President Zelenskyy said he is requesting the full terms of a short-term ceasefire Russia proposed to the United States. Washington has been pressing both sides toward a diplomatic opening.

news.title: Spain unemployment below 10% for first time since 2008; GDP outpacing Eurozone
news.text: Spain unemployment dropped to 9.9%, the first sub-10% reading since 2008, underpinned by GDP growing around 2.4% - nearly double the Eurozone average. Sovereign risk premium stands at 51 basis points.

news.title: Trump threatens trade war with Spain over Iran military base refusal
news.text: President Trump threatened to end US trade with Spain after Madrid refused to allow American forces use of joint military bases for Iran war operations. Acute strain on Spanish-American relations and a tail risk for IBEX35.

news.title: Banco Sabadell closes TSB sale to Santander; megadividend payment May 29
news.text: Banco Sabadell confirmed the closing of its sale of UK retail bank TSB to Santander, with the megadividend to shareholders set for payment on May 29.

news.title: Singapore Q1 GDP plus 4.6%; MTI upgrades 2026 forecast to 2-4%
news.text: Singapore economy grew 4.6% year-on-year in Q1 2026 driven by AI-linked tech manufacturing and services. MTI upgraded 2026 GDP forecast to 2-4% from prior 1-3% range.

news.title: Spirit Airlines ceases all operations
news.text: Spirit Airlines announced it will shut down entirely following its second bankruptcy filing since 2024, leaving passengers stranded across US airports and forcing rival carriers into emergency response.

news.title: Pentagon clears seven tech firms for classified AI deployment; Anthropic excluded
news.text: Defense Department on May 1 approved AWS, Google, Microsoft, NVIDIA, OpenAI, SpaceX, and Reflection for classified AI network deployment. Anthropic was excluded for insisting on safety guardrails in military AI use, though White House dialogue has since resumed.

day_ahead: Powell chairmanship expires May 15 - Warsh full Senate confirmation vote expected imminently. US April nonfarm payrolls on Friday May 8 (March was plus 178000, unemployment 4.3%). Iran ceasefire negotiations remain dominant macro flashpoint. Earnings this week include AMD, Pfizer, PayPal, Arista Networks, and Super Micro Computer. Anthropic 900B fundraising round expected within two weeks.

ai_news.title: Anthropic targets 900B-plus valuation in fundraising round due within two weeks
ai_news.text: Anthropic next funding round targets post-money valuation above 900 billion, surpassing OpenAI 852B valuation from its earlier 122B raise. Google has separately committed up to 40B with 10B deployed now.

ai_news.title: Pentagon clears seven AI firms for classified networks; freezes out Anthropic
ai_news.text: Defense Department on May 1 approved AWS, Google, Microsoft, NVIDIA, OpenAI, SpaceX, and Reflection for classified AI deployment. Anthropic excluded for insisting on military AI safety guardrails. White House talks have since reopened.

ai_news.title: Anthropic releases Mythos model to limited partners
ai_news.text: Anthropic quietly released its most capable model named Mythos to a limited group of partners, surpassing prior models on all internal benchmarks with significant cybersecurity applications highlighted.

ai_news.title: Google releases Gemma 4 open-weight model family
ai_news.text: Google DeepMind released Gemma 4, four open-weight models emphasising coding, agentic AI, improved reasoning, and multimodal input, intensifying the open-weight race against Meta Llama.

ai_news.title: OpenAI evaluating own smartphone to challenge Apple and Samsung
ai_news.text: OpenAI is mulling development of a proprietary smartphone for direct competition with Apple, Samsung, and Huawei at the device layer, a major strategic expansion beyond software and APIs.
